intro.title: Hello, I'm Zhengbing He
intro.desc: Professor in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, University of Nottingham Ningbo China. As a principal investigator, I have secured over 1 million US dollars in funding for national key research projects. I have published more than 180 academic papers, among which over 50 are featured in top-tier journals including Transportation Research series and IEEE Transactions. I have published papers across all sub-journals of Transportation Research (TR A/B/C/D/E/F), including more than 20 papers in TRC and 10 papers in TITS, alongside a correspondence article published in Nature. My academic metrics include over 8000 total citations, an i10-index above 100, and an H-index exceeding 40. I am an IEEE Senior Member and have been listed among the World’s Top 2% Scientists, ranking 67 out of 30588 researchers in Transportation & Logistics and 23536 across all disciplines. I currently serve as Senior Editor for IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems, Deputy Editor-in-Chief for IET Intelligent Transport Systems, Associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles, and Editorial Advisory Board Member for Transportation Research Part C. My research focuses on the integration of urban mobility, systems engineering and artificial intelligence, covering traditional research directions such as traffic flow operation and control, transportation sustainability and system resilience, as well as emerging fields including data-driven traffic modeling, autonomous driving and large language model applications. Specifically, I am a pioneer and long-term researcher in AI-enabled traffic modeling and autonomous vehicle-based traffic congestion mitigation, and one of the earliest scholars to explore generative AI applications within the transportation domain, as detailed in my academic contributions below.

item0.title: Pioneer and long-term contributor to AI-based transportation modeling
item0.desc: Car-following and lane-changing models are fundamental to exploring driver behavior and traffic dynamics, and provide core support for autonomous driving research. As early as 2014, at the initial stage of deep learning development, my research team proposed an innovative kNN-based data-driven car-following model. This work has been cited nearly 200 times, and is widely recognized as one of the earliest data-driven car-following models in review studies conducted by scholars from the University of California, Berkeley, the University of Washington and Imperial College London. In 2019, my team developed the first deep learning-based lane-changing model adopting DBN and LSTM neural networks, which has accumulated over 300 citations. It is regarded as a pioneering achievement in AI-based driver behavior and traffic dynamics modeling in numerous subsequent studies. Traffic prediction is a core research direction in transportation research and engineering practice. I led the development of the first CNN-based regional traffic speed prediction model, which has been cited more than 1600 times. Other representative achievements include spatial-temporal graph attention transformer-based trajectory prediction, graph CNN-based shared bicycle demand prediction and GAN-based travel time prediction. All relevant studies have been published in high-impact journals such as TRC, CACAIE and Information Fusion, and have gained extensive academic influence in the field of intelligent traffic prediction.

item1.title: An early innovator in large language model (LLM) applications in transportation
item1.desc: Since the widespread application of large language models began in 2023, my team has taken the lead in exploring LLM-enabled intelligent driving assistance systems, verified the optimization effect of prompt engineering on traffic prediction accuracy, and constructed AI agent models for daily travel route planning, with a series of early research outcomes published in authoritative academic journals. Our studies belong to the earliest batch of LLM application research in transportation and have attracted widespread attention from the academic community. In 2024, I was invited twice by SAE International, the world-leading authoritative institution for automotive industry standards, to deliver keynote speeches on LLM-based intelligent transportation research progress. In early 2026, my team published a correspondence article entitled AI writing tools could lead scholars from low-income countries to erase their own voices in Nature.

item2.title: Developed autonomous driving strategies to mitigate traffic congestion and guide industry development
item2.desc: To solve highway traffic congestion and vehicle emission problems, my team first proposed a feedback control-based connected vehicle congestion relief strategy and jam-absorption driving strategy. Relevant research results were published in IEEE TITS, cited over 300 times and reported by mainstream Italian media. Japanese scholars cited our jam-absorption driving strategy more than ten times in a single study, confirming that this two-stage congestion relief method is an effective solution to highway traffic jams. I am currently co-organizing a comprehensive review paper together with scholars from MIT, Georgia Tech and TU Delft, which will be published in IEEE TITS, reflecting my international academic influence in this research field. For urban traffic scenarios, I proposed an innovative lane-free autonomous driving traffic operation system and further expanded the research framework to cover pedestrian traffic scenarios. A series of related papers have been published in IEEE TIV, IEEE TVT and TRC, gaining extensive academic attention with over 100000 reading volumes on LinkedIn. Researchers from the University of Florida fully affirmed the rationality and practical value of the lane-free traffic framework, and many follow-up studies have regarded it as one of the earliest representative achievements of conflict-aware intersection traffic management. In addition, I have long been committed to the motion prediction research of surrounding traffic participants for autonomous vehicles, focusing on intersection traffic conflict scenarios, and have successively proposed prediction methods based on Transformer, chain-of-thought enhanced LLM and world model.

item3.title: In-depth research on traffic state perception and visualization to promote engineering application
item3.desc: Time-space traffic diagram is one of the most core analysis and visualization tools in traffic engineering. My team has carried out systematic research on the optimization and improvement of time-space traffic diagrams, including cell-based traffic data mapping, traffic wave-aware diagram construction, traffic emission quantitative estimation and data resolution optimization. Among them, the traffic wave-aware time-space diagram construction method has been successfully applied to the famous I-24 MOTION traffic research project in Nashville, USA, and has also been adopted independently by research teams from the United Kingdom, Sweden and China in their respective research work.

item4.title: Multidisciplinary research covering transportation resilience, sustainable mobility and travel behavior
item4.desc: My representative research achievements also include dynamic vulnerability assessment of transportation systems, mesoscopic traffic dynamics-based carbon emission estimation models, multi-day online car-hailing travel behavior analysis and experimental-based daily route selection modeling, which fully demonstrate my comprehensive research capabilities, team management experience and extensive academic influence. I have co-authored multiple highly cited review papers in the transportation field, sorting out research progress in traffic trajectory data analysis, automated vehicle-oriented traffic research and transportation system resilience, with single paper citations all exceeding 200. These review studies have provided clear research directions for peer scholars and established solid academic influence within the industry.

item5.title: Intelligent Rice Transplanting and Harvesting Robot
item5.desc: An embedded agricultural robot based on FreeRTOS for rice transplanting and automatic harvesting. It adopts DJI 3508 motors to control the lifting mechanism and omnidirectional mobile chassis, achieving positioning accuracy within ±5 mm. I participated in pneumatic structure design and hardware circuit wiring, and realized dual-robot cooperative operation functions.

item6.title: SliverWolf Desktop Quadruped Robot with Robotic Arm
item6.desc: An open-source desktop-level 12-degree-of-freedom serial quadruped robot equipped with an optional manipulator and clamping gripper. The main control unit adopts STM32F103 microcontroller and MG92R high-precision steering gear. The open-source repository contains 3D structural files, hardware circuit schematic files, bottom-layer control firmware, voice interaction firmware and posture control demos. It supports multiple control modes such as Bluetooth serial port control, inertial sensor control, key control and LD3320 voice recognition control, and realizes basic actions including walking, turning, lateral movement, crawling, interactive handshake and intelligent object grabbing.

item7.title: 3508 Quadruped Robot Drive Control Test Platform
item7.desc: A quadruped robot drive performance verification platform driven by DJI M3508 brushless motors, iteratively optimized by the MTI Robotics Team since 2022. Based on the classic F427 ROBOCON competition chassis framework, it realizes remote controller and keyboard dual control, adjustable PID motor parameters, stable static standing, gyroscope attitude balance, lidar fixed-height cruise and complete gait control logic, and has completed field gait and trajectory verification for multiple robot competition events.

item8.title: ROBOCON2024 Planar Positioning System
item8.desc: MTIPPS is a planar base positioning system customized for ROBOCON 2024 competition robots. Taking STM32F103C8T6 as the main controller, it matches RS485 absolute encoders and omnidirectional wheel coding discs to realize millimeter-level two-dimensional displacement recording, and synchronously collects Hi219 inertial attitude data. The system can upload real-time positioning and attitude data to the main control board, providing data support for chassis autonomous positioning, intelligent path planning and automatic driving tests.

item9.title: Typst Chinese Resume Template Library
item9.desc: A set of editable Typst resume templates supporting one-click export to standard PDF files. It contains multiple mainstream resume layout styles, finished PDF samples, preview pictures and matching avatar material resources. The whole development workflow is based on VS Code and Tinymist Typst plug-in to realize real-time preview. The supporting documents cover Typst command-line compilation methods, batch file export operations and common font environment configuration schemes.

item0.title: Robotics Engineer Learning Roadmap (Preview) - Lain-Ego on Zhihu
item0.desc: A systematic learning route for robotics practitioners, covering core knowledge modules such as motion control, environmental perception, embedded development and SLAM positioning, with phased learning plans and high-quality resource collections.

item1.title: A Guide to Career Paths in the Robotics Industry — for High School Students - KKR Database on Zhihu
item1.desc: A comprehensive sorting of mainstream development directions and subdivision fields in the robotics industry, helping senior high school students connect university major selection with future career planning through clear industry development layout.

item2.title: On the Organizational Maturity of University Robotics Competition Teams - KKR Database on Zhihu
item2.desc: In-depth discussion on the optimization methods of university robot competition teams in organizational operation, technical knowledge inheritance and team collaborative management, providing practical solutions for long-term stable team operation and annual competition iteration.

event11.title: Internship at Qianxing Intelligence
event11.desc: Engaged in research and development work on humanoid robot motion control and intelligent human-computer interaction modules.

event10.title: Launched LocoWiki Resource Hub
event10.desc: Built a dedicated knowledge resource platform for robot competition preparation and academic research study, integrating competition official rules, technical experience sharing, academic paper reading lists and high-quality open-source project resources.

event12.title: Launched OpenDoge Open-Source Quadruped Crowdfunding
event12.desc: Served as one of the core founding developers of the OpenDoge open-source quadruped robot project, responsible for algorithm framework construction and underlying hardware driver development. The project is scheduled for official mass delivery in late June 2026.

event1.title: Open-Sourced SliverWolf Desktop Serial-Link Quadruped
event1.desc: Officially open-sourced the self-developed SliverWolf desktop serial-link quadruped robot, which integrates voice intelligent interaction and multi-end manipulator collaborative operation functions.

event2.title: 2024 ROBOCON National Competition
event2.desc: Completed the embedded control system development of R1 competition robot based on FreeRTOS, realized high-precision positioning within ±5 mm, automatic ball launching and flexible gripper grasping functions.

event4.title: Fujian Intelligent Marine Equipment Design Competition
event4.desc: Led the development of the control system for the bionic lobster underwater robot with adjustable buoyancy, and won the grand prize in the competition.

event5.title: 2024 World Marine Equipment Conference

Represented Fujian University of Technology to participate in the exhibition, displaying self-developed bionic underwater robots, and the related achievements were reported by Xinhua News Agency and other official media.

event6.title: 2025 ROBOCON National Competition
event6.desc: Independently completed the full-stack research and development of legged competition robots. Ranked 30th nationwide in speed race, 31st in obstacle avoidance race and 34th in cross-country race, and won national second prizes in all three events.

event7.title: 2025 National Undergraduate Electronics Design Contest
event7.desc: Responsible for the hardware circuit design and physical production of the ranging system power detection module, and won the national second prize.

event8.title: Open-Sourced BRS Parallel-Link Quadruped Robot
event8.desc: Released the full set of mechanical structure drawings and underlying control source code of the self-designed BRS parallel-link quadruped robot on GitHub.

event9.title: Internship at HighTorque Robotics
event9.desc: Participated in the mechanical structure optimization, control algorithm iteration and overall system joint debugging of HTDW4438 bionic quadruped robot, covering open-source research prototype and commercial product iteration development.
